text: [S_0] ("2026-01-01", "81-year-old patient admitted to nursing home in PLACE and brought to the ER this morning after being found with right gaze deviation and left hemiplegia.", 0.0, 0.5) | [S_1] ("2026-01-01", "Past Medical History: ALLERGIES: none known RELATIVE PHONE NUMBER - lives with RELATIVE, bedridden, totally dependent in all ADL/IADL, fed with dysphagic diet - dementia - immobilization syndrome - DM2 with polyneuropathy - AF not on OAT (currently on enoxaparin 2000 U/day for renal failure Crs= 3.6 mg/dl) - CRI due to single kidney (surgically removed) - Supra-aortic vessel atheromasia - unoperated left femur fracture, February 2019 - 10/22/21 admitted for melena/black tarry stools, chronic microcytic anemia under iron therapy, refused EGDS repeatedly - vaccinated against SARS-CoV2 1 dose in December 2021 - wear Deasult brace for left humerus fracture, currently at nursing home in PLACE Admitted to the emergency room on July 15th for a urinary tract infection, with a positive SARS-CoV2 test result from a swab check.", 0.0, 1.0) | [S_2] ("2026-01-01", "Therapy: metoprololol 100 1/2 tablet x2, pantoprazole 20, lasix 25, enoxaparin 2000 units/day, insulin, rocefin Found this morning by ward staff with a right gaze deviation and left hemiplegia, and was taken to the emergency room.", 0.0, 1.0) | [S_3] ("2026-01-01", "Performed brain CT with evidence of subacute ischemia in the right lenticular capsule, likely hyperdensity in the M1-M2 passage of the right MCA.", 0.0, 1.0) | [S_4] ("2026-01-01", "Blood tests CRP= 4.4, Crs= 3.66.", 0.0, 0.5) | [S_5] ("2026-01-01", "Neurological Examination: alert, head and eyes deviated to the right, mute, left facial nerve deficit of the inferior branch.", 0.0, 0.5) | [S_6] ("2026-01-01", "Probable homonymous hemianopsia on the left side.", 0.0, 0.5) | [S_7] ("2026-01-01", "Left limb plegia.", 0.0, 0.5) | [S_8] ("2026-01-01", "Moves right limbs along the stretcher surface.", 0.0, 0.5) | [S_9] ("2026-01-01", "Possible dysphagia.", 0.0, 0.5) | [S_10] ("2026-01-01", "Conclusions: ischemia in the right middle cerebral artery territory, likely of cardioembolic origin, currently under therapy in PLACE with LMWH 2000 U for CRI (anticoagulant suspended) We recommend: - continue enoxaparin 2000 U 1 vial/day - IV hydration, fasting for probable dysphagia - follow-up brain CT tomorrow morning - as the patient is unable to return to PLACE, consider if hospitalization in a medical setting is possible, given the lack of further neurological indications in PLACE and the presence of multiple comorbidities", 0.0, 0.5)
